objective: Increase neurologist and primary-care familiarity with the diagnostic profile of Chronic Peripheral Signal Disruption (CPSD) and the role of NeuroPath as an educational reference at point of care.
content_type: 60-90s EHR sidebar education cards plus 8-12 minute on-demand CME video modules; peer-reviewed, neutral tone, fair-balance language required.
assumptions: EHR vendor partnerships already in place for at least two major systems
CME accreditation partner is identified and contracted
Targeting data sourced from vendor HCP list, not internal PHI
blockers: Final CPSD diagnostic code mapping not yet locked by Medical Affairs
CME independence requirements limit branding density inside modules
dependencies: Medical Affairs sign-off on diagnostic positioning
EHR vendor configuration for sidebar placement
open_questions: Frequency cap for EHR card exposure per HCP per week is undefined
Opt-out mechanism for HCPs declining point-of-care education is not specified
Measurement plan: engagement-only vs. downstream behavior signals not decided
Which two EHR vendors are confirmed for soft launch?
Is the CPSD analog code stable enough to drive trigger logic, or will it shift mid-flight?
risks: Off-label drift if mechanism-of-action language strays into efficacy claims at point of care
Perceived promotion in CME modules if independence boundary blurs
Fair-balance gap on short-form EHR cards due to space constraints
Audience overbreadth: combined Neurology + IM + FM may be too wide for an education-led initiative
recommended_path: EHR-triggered point-of-care education as primary; on-demand CME as secondary
